bead.title: work focus: suppress worker-start recommendation when project root is dirty
bead.description:
PROBLEM
  During the 2026-06-11 dogfood drill in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx, `ddx work focus` reported "24 bead(s) ready and no active workers detected; consider running: ddx work" while `ddx work --once` immediately exited with operator attention because the project root had uncommitted tracked frontend/e2e changes. The stewardship surface therefore recommends an action the worker preflight already knows cannot proceed.

ROOT CAUSE
  `cli/cmd/work_focus.go:136-165` builds the ready summary and worker recommendation from `store.ReadyExecution()` plus active worker count only. `cli/cmd/work_focus.go:198-215` then recommends `ddx work` whenever readyCount is nonzero and no active worker is observed. The dirty-project-root guard lives separately in the worker loop at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2096-2130`, so `ddx work focus` does not consult or mirror the preflight blocker before recommending a worker start.

PROPOSED FIX
  - In `cli/cmd/work_focus.go`, add a project-root dirty preflight check to the focus report path when projectRoot is available.
  - Surface dirty tracked paths in `unknowns` or a new operator-facing focus field, and make `WorkerRecommendation` say to resolve/commit/stash dirty paths before running `ddx work`.
  - Keep the current ready-depth reporting intact so operators still see queue pressure.
  - Add focused tests in `cli/cmd/work_focus_test.go` for dirty root suppression and clean root behavior.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not weaken the worker dirty-root guard in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`.
  - Do not auto-stash, auto-commit, or mutate the operator checkout from `ddx work focus`; it must remain read-only.
  - Do not change queue priority/rank semantics.

PARENT + DEPS
  No parent. No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestWorkFocusDirtyProjectRootSuppressesWorkerRecommendation` proves a focus report for a dirty project root does not recommend `ddx work` and includes the dirty tracked paths in an operator-visible field or recommendation.
2. `TestWorkFocusRecommendsWorkerForDeepReadyQueue` still proves a clean deep ready queue recommends `ddx work`.
3. `TestDirtyRootPreventsClaimLoop` still passes, proving the worker's existing dirty-root guard remains enforced.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkFocus(DirtyProjectRootSuppressesWorkerRecommendation|RecommendsWorkerForDeepReadyQueue)'` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestDirtyRootPreventsClaimLoop` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
